oligos.name: CEL-Seq barcoded RT primer
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription / capture primer carrying anchored poly(dT), cell barcode, 5' Illumina small-RNA adapter and T7 promoter

components.name: T7 promoter
components.role: T7 RNA polymerase promoter (drives IVT), as in Baugh et al. 2001

components.name: Cell barcode
components.role: 8-bp cell/sample barcode read at the start of read 1

components.name: Anchored poly(dT)
components.role: 24-nt poly(dT) with 3' V anchor for polyA mRNA capture

oligos.notes: Working conc. 25 ng/ul, desalted. Barcodes are 8 bp, designed in groups of four so the first five positions have equal base representation; each pair differs by >=2 nt. The 40 published barcodes (inserted at [CELL_BARCODE:8], 5'->3'): 1 CATCACGC, 2 GTCGTCGC, 3 ACGACCGC, 4 TGATGCGC, 5 CATCAATC, 6 GTCGTATC, 7 ACGACATC, 8 TGATGATC, 9 CATCATCC, 10 GTCGTTCC, 11 ACGACTCC, 12 TGATGTCC, 13 CATCAGAA, 14 GTCGTGAA, 15 ACGACGAA, 16 TGATGGAA, 17 TCACACGC, 18 CGTGTCGC, 19 GACACCGC, 20 ATGTGCGC, 21 TCACATCA, 22 CGTGTTCA, 23 GACACTCA, 24 ATGTGTCA, 25 TCACAGAG, 26 CGTGTGAG, 27 GACACGAG, 28 ATGTGGAG, 29 CTAACCGC, 30 GCTTGCGC, 31 AGCCACGC, 32 TAGGTCGC, 33 CTAACTCA, 34 GCTTGTCA, 35 AGCCATCA, 36 TAGGTTCA, 37 CTAACGAG, 38 GCTTGGAG, 39 AGCCAGAG, 40 TAGGTGAG.

oligos.name: Illumina 5' RNA adapter (RA5)
oligos.role: 5' Illumina small-RNA adapter; supplied within the CEL-Seq RT primer
oligos.notes: 5' Illumina adapter 'as used in the Illumina small RNA kit'; this segment is embedded in the CEL-Seq RT primer, so its sequence is documented directly.

oligos.name: Illumina 3' RNA adapter (RA3)
oligos.role: 3' Illumina small-RNA adapter ligated to fragmented aRNA
oligos.notes: TruSeq Small RNA RA3 adapter; protocol uses it diluted 1:5. Sequence cited in the protocol as 'available from Illumina' and not printed there; shown is the standard Illumina TruSeq Small RNA RA3 sequence.

oligos.name: RNA RT Primer (RTP)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer complementary to the ligated 3' adapter (RA3)
oligos.notes: From the Illumina TruSeq Small RNA kit; primes SuperScript II RT of the RA3-ligated RNA. Sequence not printed in the protocol ('available from Illumina'); shown is the standard TruSeq Small RNA RTP.

oligos.name: RNA PCR Primer (RP1)
oligos.role: Forward library PCR primer (adds P5 and completes the 5' adapter)
oligos.notes: From the Illumina TruSeq Small RNA kit. Sequence not printed in the protocol ('available from Illumina'); shown is the standard TruSeq Small RNA RP1.

oligos.name: Indexed RNA PCR Primer (RPIX)
oligos.role: Reverse indexed library PCR primer (adds i7 sample index and P7)

components.name: i7 sample index
components.role: 6-nt Illumina library index (read as separate index read)

oligos.notes: RPIX = uniquely indexed reverse PCR primer from the Illumina TruSeq Small RNA kit; provides an additional per-library barcode so multiple IVT pools run together. Sequence not printed in the protocol ('available from Illumina'); shown is the standard TruSeq Small RNA RPI scaffold.

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: P5 flow-cell binding sequence (5' end of final library)
oligos.notes: Introduced via RP1; canonical Illumina P5.

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: P7 flow-cell binding sequence (3' end of final library)
oligos.notes: Introduced via RPIX; canonical Illumina P7. Appears in the final library as its reverse complement ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG.

final_library.source_label: CEL-Seq final Illumina (TruSeq Small RNA) library
annotation_lines: GTTCAGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC = Illumina 5' RNA adapter (RA5) / read 1 region
[CDNA] = cDNA insert (3'-most fragment of the transcript)
AACTCCAGTCAC = TruSeq Small RNA read 2 / RP linker

evidence.locator: CEL-Seq final Illumina (TruSeq Small RNA) library

library_generation.title: Single-cell lysis, primer annealing & first-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: The barcoded CEL-Seq primer captures the cell's polyA mRNA and ArrayScript reverse transcriptase copies it into first-strand cDNA.
library_generation.note: Each frozen single cell receives one uniquely barcoded CEL-Seq RT primer (+ERCC spike-in), is heated to open the cell and anneal the anchored poly(dT), then reverse transcribed at 42C (Ambion MessageAmp II ArrayScript).
library_generation.product:
T7 promoter                RA5 (5' adapter)          BC        poly(dT)
5'- CGATTGAGGCCGGTAATACGACTCACTATAGGGGTTCAGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC[CELL_BARCODE:8]TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTV[CDNA]------->
                                                                                      3'- ...AAAAAAAAAA[mRNA] -5'

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: RNaseH + DNA Pol I convert the cDNA:mRNA hybrid into double-stranded cDNA carrying a functional T7 promoter.
library_generation.note: Standard second-strand reaction at 16C yields dsDNA; the T7 promoter is now double-stranded and transcription-competent.

library_generation.title: Pooling + T7 in vitro transcription (linear amplification)
library_generation.summary: Barcoded double-stranded cDNAs from many cells are pooled and linearly amplified in one round of T7 IVT into antisense amplified RNA (aRNA).
library_generation.note: Pooling reaches the ~400 pg IVT input threshold; a single 13 hr IVT reaction makes many antisense RNA copies per molecule, each retaining the barcode and 5' adapter.

library_generation.title: RNA fragmentation
library_generation.summary: aRNA is chemically fragmented to a sequencing-appropriate size, peaking around 500 nt.
library_generation.note: Fragmentation (Tris-acetate/KOAc/MgOAc, 94C, 3 min) breaks aRNA; only the 5'-most fragment still carries the barcode + RA5 adapter.
library_generation.product:
5'- GGGUUCAGAGUUCUACAGUCCGACGAUC[CELL_BARCODE:8]UUUUUUUUUUUU[frag] -3'   (barcode-bearing fragment)
5'- [internal aRNA fragments, no adapter] -3'                            (not retained in final library)

library_generation.title: End repair (Antarctic phosphatase + PNK)
library_generation.summary: Fragment ends are dephosphorylated then re-phosphorylated to give 5'-monophosphate / 3'-OH ends compatible with adapter ligation.
library_generation.note: Antarctic phosphatase removes 3' phosphates/cyclic phosphates; T4 PNK adds a 5' phosphate, priming the RNA for T4 RNA ligase 2 (truncated).

library_generation.title: 3' adapter (RA3) ligation
library_generation.summary: The Illumina 3' small-RNA adapter (RA3) is ligated onto the 3' end of the aRNA fragments.
library_generation.note: T4 RNA Ligase 2 (truncated) attaches RA3 (diluted 1:5) to 3'-OH ends; only fragments with both the 5' (RA5, from the primer) and 3' (RA3) adapters become amplifiable.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription (RTP primer)
library_generation.summary: The RNA RT primer (RTP) anneals to the ligated RA3 adapter and SuperScript II copies the adapter-flanked RNA into cDNA.
library_generation.note: RTP is complementary to RA3; RT at 50C generates a cDNA copy that is now the PCR template.

library_generation.title: Library PCR (RP1 + indexed RPIX) & bead cleanup
library_generation.summary: 12 cycles of PCR with RP1 (P5) and an indexed RPIX (i7 + P7) complete both flow-cell adapters and add the library index, producing the final dsDNA library.
library_generation.note: RP1 adds P5/completes RA5; RPIX adds the 6-nt i7 index and P7. Two AMPure XP cleanups yield a ~300-400 bp library.

library_sequencing.primer: Illumina small-RNA Read 1 sequencing primer (sequence = RA5; anneals to the bottom strand across the 5' RNA adapter)
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8-bp cell barcode immediately followed by the anchored poly(T) stretch (>=15 bases were read for read 1); >95% of reads carry the barcode precisely at the start of read 1, invariably followed by poly(T).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer (= RA5) anneals to bottom strand; reads cell barcode then poly(T):
5'-...GTTCAGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC----------------->            (read 1, 5'->3')
5'-...GTTCAGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATCNNNNNNNNTTTTTTT...[cDNA]-3'   (top)
3'-...CAAGTCTCAAGATGTCAGGCTGCTAGNNNNNNNNAAAAAAA...[cDNA]-5'   (bottom = template)
                              ^cell BC(8)^ ^poly(T)^

library_sequencing.primer: Illumina i7 index sequencing primer (anneals across the RP/RA3 linker on the top strand, 3' end abutting the index)
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 6-nt library-level index carried on the indexed RNA PCR primer (RPIX); used to demultiplex multiple IVT pools sequenced on the same lane. Read is only taken when multiplexing IVT pools.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index primer anneals over RP linker; reads the 6-nt sample index:
5'-...TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGGAACTCCAGTCAC------>                        (index read, 5'->3')
5'-...TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGGAACTCCAGTCACNNNNNNATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'  (top)
3'-...ACCTTAAGAGCCCACGGTTCCTTGAGGTCAGTGNNNNNNTAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'  (bottom = template)
                                        ^i7 index(6)^

library_sequencing.primer: Illumina small-RNA Read 2 sequencing primer (sequence complementary to RA3; anneals to the top strand across the 3' RNA adapter)
library_sequencing.note: Reads 50 bases into the 3'-most cDNA fragment, identifying the transcript strand-specifically (>97% of exonic reads sense-oriented; reads map to transcript 3' ends).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 2 primer anneals over RA3 on top strand; reads into 3'-most cDNA fragment (antisense):
                                 3'-ACCTTAAGAGCCCACGGTTCC-5'                 (Read 2 primer)
                                 <------(read 2)
5'-...TTTTTTT[.....cDNA insert.....]TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGGAACTCCAGTCAC...-3'  (top = template)
3'-...AAAAAAA[.....cDNA insert.....]ACCTTAAGAGCCCACGGTTCCTTGAGGTCAGTG...-5'  (bottom)

title: CEL-Seq
description: CEL-Seq (Cell Expression by Linear amplification and Sequencing) is a multiplexed single-cell RNA-seq method that barcodes each cell's mRNA during reverse transcription, pools the samples, and linearly amplifies them with a single round of T7 in vitro transcription (IVT) instead of PCR. Each cell's RT primer carries an anchored poly(dT), an 8-bp cell barcode, the Illumina 5' small-RNA adapter, and a T7 promoter. After IVT the amplified antisense RNA is fragmented and built into an Illumina directional (TruSeq Small RNA) library. Paired-end sequencing reads the cell barcode followed by a poly(T) stretch in read 1 and the 3'-most mRNA fragment in read 2, yielding strand-specific, 3'-end digital gene-expression counts.

original_publication.title: CEL-Seq: Single-Cell RNA-Seq by Multiplexed Linear Amplification

authors.name: Tamar Hashimshony
authors.affiliation: Department of Biology, Technion-Israel Institute of Technology, Haifa 32000, Israel

authors.name: Florian Wagner
authors.affiliation: Department of Biology, Technion-Israel Institute of Technology, Haifa 32000, Israel

authors.name: Noa Sher
authors.affiliation: Department of Biology, Technion-Israel Institute of Technology, Haifa 32000, Israel

authors.name: Itai Yanai
authors.affiliation: Department of Biology, Technion-Israel Institute of Technology, Haifa 32000, Israel

throughput.summary: Amplification of up to ~50 cells per person per day; ~10 IVT pools (~500 cells) converge into a single library preparation. The 8-bp barcode design makes the number of uniquely barcoded samples per IVT essentially unlimited.
throughput.rna: IVT requires ~400 pg total RNA input; RT works down to ~5 pg (single mammalian cell). 10 pg C. elegans RNA ~ 280,000 mRNA molecules.
throughput.dna: Final library ~1 ng/ul, size peak ~300-400 bp

publication.statistical_model: Absolute molecule counts obtained by least-squares linear regression on ERCC spike-in expression levels; per-gene expression reported as transcripts per million (tpm), with differential expression tested by FDR-corrected t tests.

other.label: Amplification
other.value: Single round of linear T7 in vitro transcription (IVT), not PCR

other.label: Strand specificity
other.value: >98% of exonic reads from the sense strand

other.label: Barcoding efficiency
other.value: >96% of reads contain a barcode (95.5% at start of read 1)

other.label: Kits
other.value: Ambion MessageAmp II aRNA (AM1751) + Illumina TruSeq Small RNA sample prep (RS-200-0012)

data_processing.summary: A custom multistep pipeline run in the Galaxy framework: paired-end reads are trimmed/filtered, demultiplexed by the first 8 bases of read 1 (the cell barcode), read 2 is mapped to the reference genome, reads are counted per gene and normalized to transcripts per million (tpm). Because CEL-Seq retains a single 3'-most fragment per transcript, counts require no gene-length normalization; absolute mRNA copy numbers are then obtained by least-squares linear regression on the ERCC spike-in values.
data_processing.statistical_model: Least-squares linear regression of tpm-normalized counts against known ERCC spike-in concentrations to recover absolute mRNA copy numbers; no gene-length normalization is applied because CEL-Seq retains a single 3'-most fragment per transcript.
